title: Product Manager - Fleet Management
desc:
We are the movers of the world and the makers of the future. We get up every day, roll up our sleeves and build a better world -- together. At Ford, we’re all a part of something bigger than ourselves. Are you ready to change the way the world moves?

Launched in 2023, Integrated Services creates and markets new customer experiences by integrating hardware, software and services across Ford Blue, Model e and Ford Pro. These products and services allow Ford's retail and commercial customers to customize their vehicles like never before with OTA (over-the-air)-driven experiences that grow better over time. Ford is developing leadership at the forefront of connected, digital platforms that will revolutionize every aspect of how vehicles function, the way people interact with them, and the value those vehicles provide.

In this position...

As a Product Manager on the Fleet Management product team, you will be responsible for creating, managing, and reporting on a defined set of metrics. You will be working with other product managers, engineers, sales and operations teams to create a cohesive story using product and user data. You will be responsible for creating and maintaining dashboards, product tracking and tagging and for a large portfolio of revenue-generating products.

_This is a hybrid position requiring an onsite schedule of 4 days a week in Dearborn, MI. The selected candidate that lives within 50 miles of Dearborn, MI will be required to work onsite for a minimum of 4 days a week._ _This is not a remote position, and relocation is not offered._

What you'll do...

Product Strategy:

+ Define requirements for product analytics dashboards that focus on current business state, including subscriptions, transactional maintenance data and comprehensive fleet information

+ Collaborate with business operations to define key metrics and what achievable targets are

+ Partner with data engineering to identify new reportable data sources within our existing data ecosystem

User Experience:

+ Collaborate with engineering and design to develop a world-class user-experience

+ Bring data to life for fleet management operations team by partnering with design to create digital data experiences for business and product

Cross-Functional Collaboration:

+ Collaborate with data engineering to ensure products meet technical requirements and design excellence

+ Work closely with account management and operations to report on business initiatives

+ Facilitate communications and trainings for operations and account management teams when deploying new products to ensure alignment and clarity

Data-Driven Decision Making:

+ Utilize customer data to make informed product decisions, including usage and NPS data

+ Identify and track key performance indicators (KPIs), while providing regular stakeholder updates on performance and customer satisfaction

Product Launch & Lifecycle Management:

+ Monitor product performance and customer feedback to identify areas of improvement that will influence future product roadmaps

You'll have...

+ Bachelor’s degree required

+ 3+ years of product management experience

+ Experience working with Google BigQuery, Amplitude or other SaaS analytics platforms

+ Familiarity with software tools like JIRA, Figma, Google Cloud, GitHub, etc.

+ Must be willing and able to work onsite for a minimum of 4 days a week in Dearborn, MI. This is not a remote position, and relocation is not offered.

Even better, you may have...

+ Demonstrated experience win building dashboards or other data reporting mechanisms

+ Ability to work in a highly-matrix environment and manage multiple products across the ecosystem

You may not check every box, or your experience may look a little different from what we've outlined, but if you think you can bring value to Ford Motor Company, we encourage you to apply!

As an established global company, we offer the benefit of choice. You can choose what your Ford future will look like: will your story span the globe, or keep you close to home? Will your career be a deep dive into what you love, or a series of new teams and new skills? Will you be a leader, a changemaker, a technical expert, a culture builder…or all of the above? No matter what you choose, we offer a work life that works for you, including:

+ Immediate medical, dental, vision and prescription drug coverage

+ Flexible family care days, paid parental leave, new parent ramp-up programs, subsidized back-up childcare and more

+ Family building benefits including adoption and surrogacy expense reimbursement, fertility treatments, and more

+ Vehicle discount program for employees and family members and management leases

+ Tuition assistance

+ Established and active employee resource groups

+ Paid time off for individual and team community service

+ A generous schedule of paid holidays, including the week between Christmas and New Year’s Day

+ Paid time off and the option to purchase additional vacation time.

For more information on salary and benefits, click here: https://fordcareers.co/GSRSP1

This position is a range of salary grades 7-8.

Visa sponsorship is not available for this position.

Candidates for positions with Ford Motor Company must be legally authorized to work in the United States. Verification of employment eligibility will be required at the time of hire.

We are an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, religion, color, age, sex, national origin, sexual orientation, gender identity, disability status or protected veteran status. In the United States, if you need a reasonable accommodation for the online application process due to a disability, please call 1-888-336-0660.

Onsite work of up to four days per week may be required for candidates within commuting distance of a Ford hub location.

This is a hybrid position requiring an onsite schedule of 4 days a week in Dearborn, MI. The selected candidate that lives within 50 miles of Dearborn, MI will be required to work onsite for a minimum of 4 days a week. This is not a remote position, and relocation is not offered.

title: Travel Product Manager
desc:
It’s a great time to join AAA The Auto Club Group!

JOIN THE TEAM COMMITTED TO DRIVING YOUR CAREER FORWARD

Job Type:

Full time

Exempt/Non Exempt:

Salary

Job Description:
• This position is able to work remotely but will require frequent travel based on seasonality, industry and corporate events both domestic and internationally.

Travel Product Manager - The Auto Club Group

What you will do:

Provides leadership in the strategic positioning of Auto Club Group (ACG) Travel products and services in order to meet member needs, build company revenues and meet corporate objectives.

Responsible for strategy and development of travel products in a manner that supports growth of AAA Travel Agency strategies and objectives, including:
• Tours
• Cruises
• ACG developed group travel products
• AAA Joint Ventures or club owned products, including AAA Exclusive Vacations, Pleasant Holidays, AAA Member Choice Vacations
• AAA Vacations in consultation with AAA National work groups
• Travel Insurance
• Strategic partners, such as Hertz
• Member travel experience enhancements, such as excursions and discounts

Manage select key travel supplier accounts, with responsibility for annual planning, program development, and performance.

Build annual plans to grow ACG results in alignment with our strategies and partner priorities, manage relationships with our travel partners to secure increasing levels of co-op marketing funds and overrides. Assess performance and identify actions that will grow ACG’s sales results with our targeted segments and overrides to maximize ACG profitability and deliver planned P&L results.

Act as a travel product subject matter expert to support AAA Travel Agency sales development activities.

Represent ACG on Travel Partner Advisory Boards and represent ACG strategically on AAA National work groups and task forces, driving ACG leadership in the Federation.

Supervisory Responsibilities:

No direct reports, but manages key Travel accounts as well as relationships with various Travel vendors.

How you will benefit:
• A competitive annual salary between $100,000 - $115,000 with annual bonus potential based on performance
• ACG offers excellent and comprehensive benefits packages, including:
• Medical, dental and vision benefits
• 401k Match
• Paid parental leave and adoption assistance
• Paid Time Off (PTO), company paid holidays, CEO days, and floating holidays
• Paid volunteer day annually
• Tuition assistance program, professional certification reimbursement program and other professional development opportunities
• AAA Membership
• Discounts, perks, and rewards and much more

Required Qualifications (these Are The Minimum Requirements To Qualify)

Education (include minimum education and any licensing/certifications):
• High school diploma.
• Possession of a valid State Driver’s License

Experience:
• Significant experience in developing and implementing travel products
• Planning, developing and maintaining client relationships
• Preparing and presenting proposals
• Leading cross-functional engagement to meet or exceed sales goals and objectives

Knowledge and Skills:
• Ability to work in a self-directed manner to identify, plan and develop travel products that drive sales and profit growth
• Excellent verbal, written and presentation skills
• Working Knowledge of Microsoft Office applications for word processing, spreadsheets and presentations
• ACG products and services
• Marketing and sales techniques
• Deep knowledge of travel industry trends, practices and travel regulations (ARC, IATAN)
• Travel industry and supplier relationships
• Financial management principles required for planning profitable growth

Preferred Qualifications
• Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Finance, Marketing, Sales, or Hospitality
• Preferred 5 years of experience in the travel and tourism industry with a specific emphasis on cruises
• Prior experience with Salesforce and CRM tools
• Proficient in Excel and PowerPoint
• Strong background in financial management through reporting, assessment, analysis, and recommendations regarding supplier performance within assigned portfolio.
• Experience communicating financials into actionable plans to drive growth
• Understanding of how to utilize data to make informed decisions
• Strong written and oral communication skills

Work Environment

This position is able to work remotely from a home office location but will require frequent travel based on seasonality, industry and corporate events both domestic and internationally. This is subject to change based on amendments and/or modifications to the ACG Flex Work policy.

Who We Are

Become a part of something bigger.

The Auto Club Group (ACG) provides membership, travel, insurance, and financial service offerings to approximately 14+ million members and customers across 14 states and 2 U.S. territories through AAA, Meemic, and Fremont brands. ACG belongs to the national AAA federation and is the second largest AAA club in North America.

By continuing to invest in more advanced technology, pursuing innovative products, and hiring a highly skilled workforce, AAA continues to build upon its heritage of providing quality service and helping our members enjoy life’s journey through insurance, travel, financial services, and roadside assistance.

And when you join our team, one of the first things you’ll notice is that same, whole-hearted, enthusiastic advocacy for each other.

We have positions available for every walk of life! AAA prides itself on creating an inclusive and welcoming environment of diverse backgrounds, experiences, and viewpoints, realizing our differences make us stronger.

To learn more about AAA The Auto Club Group visit www.aaa.com

Important Note:

ACG’s Compensation philosophy is to provide a market-competitive structure of fair, equitable and performance-based pay to attract and retain excellent talent that will enable ACG to meet its short and long-term goals. ACG utilizes a geographic pay differential as part of the base salary compensation program. Pay ranges outlined in this posting are based on the various ranges within the geographic areas which ACG operates. Salary at time of offer is determined based on these and other factors as associated with the job and job level.

The above statements describe the principal and essential functions, but not all functions that may be inherent in the job. This job requires the ability to perform duties contained in the job description for this position, including, but not limited to, the above requirements. Reasonable accommodations will be made for otherwise qualified applicants, as needed, to enable them to fulfill these requirements.

The Auto Club Group, and all its affiliated companies, is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, disability or protected veteran status.

Regular and reliable attendance is essential for the function of this job.

AAA The Auto Club Group is committed to providing a safe workplace. Every applicant offered employment within The Auto Club Group will be required to consent to a background and drug screen based on the requirements of the position.

title: Data & Analytics Product Manager
desc:
Amerisure creates exceptional value for its partners, policyholders, and employees. As a property and casualty insurance company, Amerisure's promise to our partner agencies and policyholders begins with a comprehensive line of insurance products designed to protect businesses, as well as the health and safety of every employee. With an A.M. Best "A" (Excellent) rating, Amerisure serves mid-sized commercial enterprises focused in construction, manufacturing and healthcare. Ranked as one of the top 100 Property & Casualty companies in the United States, we proudly manage nearly $1 Billion of Direct Written Premium and maintain $1.21 billion in surplus.

Amerisure is Growing! Join Our Technology Team. Amerisure is excited to announce a new opportunity on our technology team! We are seeking a Data & Analytics Product Manager to help shape the future of data-driven decision-making within our organization. This role will be hybrid, working two days a week in the office and three days the last week of the month. The ideal person will possess the following skill set.

Summary Statement

The Data, Analytics, and AI Product Manager is responsible for driving the strategy, governance, and implementation of enterprise data and analytics products. This role acts as a critical liaison across business and technology stakeholders to develop and deliver a comprehensive roadmap that advances data-driven decision-making, accelerates AI adoption, and ensures robust data governance. This role is pivotal in translating business needs into scalable data solutions, fostering innovation, and maximizing business impact.

Essential Tasks/Major Duties
• Lead the development and execution of the data, analytics, and AI product roadmap in partnership with business units and IT.
• Champion the integration of advanced analytics and AI capabilities into business processes, identifying opportunities for automation and insight generation.
• Establish and maintain data governance frameworks, ensuring data quality, security, and compliance with regulatory requirements
• Oversee the entire product lifecycle, including requirements gathering, end to end architecture/design, development, testing, integration, and iteration.
• Work across the organization to align product strategies with business goals, manage resources, and facilitate effective collaboration between product teams and other departments to drive product success and customer satisfaction.
• Collaborate with data scientists, engineers, and business partners to deliver high-value analytics and AI solutions.
• Define and track key performance indicators (KPIs) to measure product success and business impact.
• Drive change management and user adoption strategies for new data and AI products.
• Communicate complex data and AI concepts to diverse audiences, ensuring clarity and alignment.

Knowledge, Skills & Abilities
• Bachelor's degree, preferably in Computer Science, Information Technology, Computer
• Engineering, or related IT discipline; or equivalent experience.
• 6 years of experience in a data analytics or working in an Information Technology field supporting Data Analytics/Engineering.
• 2 years product management experience, including defining, developing and launching successful products.
• 3 years of experience utilizing SQL, data modeling principles and data architecture.
• 2 years of experience leading teams.
• Strong critical thinking, analysis, and problem-solving skills.
• Strong written and oral communication skills.
• Ability to analyze data, extract insights, and translate them into actionable strategies.
• Experience partnering with cross-functional business stakeholders to convey complex concepts and translate them to different audience.
• Familiarity with big data technologies.
• Knowledge of machine learning algorithms, data mining techniques, statistical modeling, and other relevant technologies.

title: Product Manager, CRM
desc:
About the Company:
At Playlist, life's richest moments happen when people step away from screens to move, connect, explore, and play. We're building the definitive platform for intentional living, connecting people with inspiring experiences in fitness, wellness, and beyond. With popular brands like Mindbody and ClassPass, Playlist empowers businesses and individuals, making it effortless for aspirations to become actions. Join us in reshaping technology's role to foster meaningful, real-world connections.The Opportunity
As the Product Manager, CRM and Lifecycle, you will own lifecycle management functions across all products in our B2B2C enterprise platform. You will lead a development team and partner with design, engineering, data, marketing, sales, and customer success to deliver scalable CRM capabilities across email, SMS, and automated marketing journeys
Responsibilities

Own the CRM and lifecycle roadmap and backlog across all products in our B2B2C enterprise platform
Manage our enterprise CRM product capabilities, including email, SMS, automated marketing journeys, and lifecycle management tools
Lead a development team in an Agile environment and drive predictable, high-quality delivery
Conduct customer discovery with enterprise clients and end users to identify problems, validate solutions, and define the value to be delivered
Write user stories and acceptance criteria, and partner with design and engineering to ship end-to-end lifecycle experiences
Prioritize feature development using impact versus effort, and clearly communicate tradeoffs and sequencing
Own go-to-market documentation and support materials, and partner on launch readiness and enablement for sales and customer success
Define success metrics and own post-launch measurement to track performance and drive iteration once in market
Own communication compliance and governance across channels, including TCPA, CAN SPAM, CCPA, consent and preference management, and opt-in and opt-out requirements
Use Jira and Atlassian tools to manage delivery, status reporting, and stakeholder communication

Qualifications

5 plus years of product management experience delivering software in an Agile environment
Experience owning CRM, lifecycle, or marketing automation products, including SMS and email platforms, and automated journeys
Strong customer research skills and ability to translate insights into clear requirements, user stories, and acceptance criteria
Proven prioritization and stakeholder management skills, including impact versus effort decision-making
Comfortable partnering cross-functionally with engineering, design, data, legal or compliance, and go-to-market teams
Experience with Jira and Atlassian tools
Bachelor’s degree preferred

Pay Transparency

title: Group Product Manager (Remote)
desc:
Who We Are

Join a team that puts its People First! As a member of First American’s family of companies, Data & Analytics is a national provider of property-centric information, analytics, risk management and valuation solutions. First American maintains and curates the industry’s largest property and ownership dataset with over 7 billion document images. Our major platforms and products include: DataTree®, FraudGuard®, RegsData™, TaxSource™ and ACI®. The First American Data & Analytics division boasts more than 20 patents and remains at the forefront of innovation - leveraging technology and data to deliver best-in-class decisioning solutions. Fueled by our industry-leading data and using our technology and proprietary process, our solutions provide lenders, real estate and title companies with actionable insights - enabling them to make better, increasingly automated, decisions. With offices in all major metropolitan areas, including California and New York, DNA teams work collaboratively from across the country. Since 1889, First American (NYSE: FAF) has held an unwavering belief in its people. They are passionate about what they do, and we are equally passionate about fostering an environment where all feel welcome, supported, and empowered to be innovative and reach their full potential. Our inclusive, people-first culture has earned our company numerous accolades, including being named to the Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For® list for ten consecutive years. We have also earned awards as a best place to work for women, diversity and LGBTQ+ employees, and have been included on more than 50 regional best places to work lists. First American will always strive to be a great place to work, for all. For more information, please visit www.careers.firstam.com.

What We Do

The Group Product Manager will lead the strategy, development, and execution of title data and workflow products within DataTrace. This role is responsible for shaping the product vision and roadmap, aligning cross-functional teams, and delivering solutions that materially improve the accuracy, efficiency, and reliability of title solutions. This is a high-impact role operating in an accuracy-critical, regulated environment. The Group Product Manager will apply modern data and AI-assisted capabilities to evolve title products while ensuring trust, compliance, and operational effectiveness. This role is intended for a senior product leader with prior experience in the title industry or closely adjacent public-records or regulated data domains. The ideal candidate has owned complex data products end-to-end and can operate with minimal ramp up time. This is a hands-on, player-coach role. While responsible for leading a product team, this leader is expected to remain deeply involved in complex product decisions, prioritization, and system-level tradeoffs.Job Description

What You’ll Do – Product Leadership & Strategy
• Own and evolve the product vision and multi-year roadmap for title data and workflow products in alignment with company strategy and customer needs.
• Define the strategy for expanding title data platforms, including ingestion, integration, and normalization across diverse public-record and title data sources.
• Chart the course for the future of the business with modern data, AI-assisted workflows, data extraction, and workflow automation.
• Prioritize initiatives that improve data quality, scalability, operational efficiency, and overall business impact.
• Identify new opportunities in the real estate, title, and data landscape to expand market share and customer value across the title lifecycle.
• Partner with executive leadership on product direction, financial and strategic objectives, and go-to-market decisions.

Execution & Delivery
• Lead the product team to build and deliver production-grade title data and workflow solutions.
• Collaborate with engineering, data, and design teams to deliver scalable, reliable systems and workflows.
• Ensure clarity and alignment around product requirements, success metrics, and feedback loops to drive continuous improvement.
• Define and operationalize quality standards and review processes that improve trust and reduce downstream friction.
• Guide vendor and partner strategies related to data providers, platforms, and supporting services.

Customer & Market Focus
• Engage with title insurers, agents, underwriters, abstractors, and operational stakeholders to understand workflows, pain points, and opportunities.
• Stay current on title industry practices, policy structures, and regulatory considerations to ensure product relevance and differentiation.
• Translate insights into actionable product strategies that drive adoption, retention, and expansion, focusing on measurable outcomes.

Team Development & Leadership
• Mentor and grow a high-performing product team, including product managers and domain-aligned subject matter experts.
• Champion a customer-first mindset and pragmatic product practices across the organization.
• Drive alignment across departments to ensure smooth go-to-market, customer onboarding, and ongoing enablement for title-specific workflows.

What You’ll Bring – Must Haves
• 10+ years of experience in product management, with 1–3+ years leading teams of product managers.
• Proven track record of delivering complex, high-impact products and leading cross-functional teams.
• Ability to set long-term product strategy while remaining closely engaged in execution and delivery.
• Strong problem-solving mindset with a willingness to roll up your sleeves and stay closely involved in execution and iteration.
• Data-driven decision making, with experience defining and analyzing metrics that inform product direction and prioritization.
• Strong written and verbal communication skills with the ability to influence and align diverse stakeholders around product strategy, priorities, and tradeoffs.

Domain Experience (Title & Data Products)
• This role requires prior experience operating in the title industry or closely adjacent public-records or regulated data domains. Successful candidates will be comfortable working with complex, accuracy-critical data products that support real-world operational workflows.
• Hands-on experience working in or closely with the title ecosystem, such as a title insurer, large agent, data provider, or title-focused technology platform.
• Working understanding of how title and public-records data is structured, indexed, and maintained, including how jurisdictional variation in recorder practices and document standards impacts data quality, search behavior, and chain-of-title outcomes.
• Experience owning or contributing to document- or record-based data products, particularly where data is derived from semi-structured or unstructured sources and used in operational or decision-support workflows.
• Familiarity with core title data challenges such as linking parties, properties, and records over time, and supporting confident decisions when data is incomplete, inconsistent, or ambiguous.
• Comfort operating in accuracy-critical and regulated environments where product decisions have direct downstream operational, financial, or compliance implications.
• Familiarity with title policy structures and operational handoffs across search, examination, clearance, and closing.

Nice to Haves
• Background in real estate, geospatial data, risk modeling, or large-scale public-records platforms.
• Experience with B2B SaaS or data platforms serving institutional clients.
• Exposure to modern AI/ML techniques applied to data-intensive or workflow-driven domains.
• Familiarity with industry trends related to title, eRecording, or adjacent real estate technologies.
• MBA or advanced degree a plus, but not required.

Additional Details
• Reporting Line: Reports to Director, Title Products, Division Product Management.
• Location / Work Mode: Hybrid in Santa Ana, CA, or remote with periodic travel.
• Travel: Approximately 10–20%.
• * Note that the following statements only apply to candidates who will be working from an unincorporated area within Los Angeles County. **
First American will consider for employment all qualified applicants, including those with arrest or conviction records, in a manner consistent with the requirements of applicable state and local laws (e.g., the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers and the California Fair Chance Act).
First American intends to conduct a review of an applicant’s criminal history in connection with a conditional offer. First American reasonably believes that a criminal history may have a direct, adverse and negative relationship with the following material job duties for this position potentially resulting in the withdrawal of the conditional offer of employment: handling of confidential, proprietary or trade secret information belonging to First American or its customers, administrating or facilitating financial transactions, and the ability to meet customer-imposed criminal history requirements.

What We Offer

By choice, we don’t simply accept individuality – we embrace it, we support it, and we thrive on it! Our People First Culture celebrates diversity, equity and inclusion not simply because it’s the right thing to do, but also because it’s the key to our success. We are proud to foster an authentic and inclusive workplace For All. You are free and encouraged to bring your entire, unique self to work. First American is an equal opportunity employer in every sense of the term.

Based on eligibility, First American offers a comprehensive benefits package including medical, dental, vision, 401k, PTO/paid sick leave and other great benefits like an employee stock purchase plan.

title: Staff Product Manager - US (Remote)
desc:
Luxury Presence is building the AI growth platform for real estate. Backed by Bessemer Venture Partners and other top investors, we're a Series C company on track to hit $100M in annual recurring revenue in the next six months. More than 87,000 real estate professionals, including over 30% of the WSJ Real Trends top 100 agents in the United States, use us to run and grow their business.

Why this role

This role will lead product for a new team inside our Flagship Group to accelerate Luxury Presence’s shift to an AI-powered platform for real estate growth. Flagship owns the technology at the core of our business: stunning real estate websites and the foundational elements of Presence, our platform that enables clients and internal teams to create websites, integrate with CRMs, create beautiful presentations for home buyers and sellers, and much more.

As a Staff Product Manager, you’ll own strategy, roadmap, execution, and rollout high-impact, high-complexity projects, including new offerings and strengthening our existing solutions. Working in a fast-paced environment with a skilled designer and team of fully AI-equipped engineers, you’ll shape the core product that our customers and internal teams rely on every day — and see your work unlock momentum across the company.

If you want to build high-value, intuitive, beautiful products at an AI-forward start-up — this is your role.

What You’ll Do
• Own a roadmap that increases customer value, raises quality, and removes friction across Presence.
• Turn ambiguous problems into a sharp plans with clear value, scope, milestones, and metrics
• Run discovery with customers and internal teams. Validate problems, test solutions, and de-risk early.
• Write crisp PRDs, roadmaps, and decision docs that drive clarity and align stakeholders
• Sequence work for iterative delivery and fast learning
• Collaborate with Design and Engineering to ship reliable, intuitive, and delightful experiences.
• Define and analyze success metrics to understand if your bets are paying off and guide future work

What We’re Looking For
• 5+ years in product management, including 2–3 years leading large, complex 0→1 or major revamp initiatives
• Startup mindset — you thrive in ambiguity, are a master at finding the MVP, and balance speed with quality
• Exceptional communicator & collaborator — you drive clarity and gain trust and buy-in within your team and across the business
• Technical product track record — you’re experienced building technical products such as integrations and APIs
• Passion for quality — you care deeply about building exceptional user experiences and nailing the details
• Fluent in AI tools — you’re an expert at leveraging AI tools to multiply your impact

Nice to Haves
• Experience building AI-powered products – agentic workflows, LLM selection, prompt creation, and evaluation suites
• Familiarity with the real estate industry

Why You’ll Love It Here
• 🚀 Join a product-led, design- and AI-forward company with real traction and a clear growth strategy
• 🎯 Drive innovation on core functionality and greenfield bets
• 💡 Work with a talented, humble, and highly collaborative team
• 🌍 Thrive in a remote-first, async-friendly environment
• 🌱 Grow into leadership as we expand the platform and team

$170,000 - $210,000 a year

Join us in shaping the future of real estate

The real estate industry is in the midst of a seismic shift, and the future belongs to those who break new ground. As one of the fastest-growing companies in the proptech and marketing sectors, Luxury Presence challenges the status quo of what technology can do for real estate agents, leaders, and brokerages.

We're a team of agile and tenacious innovators working collaboratively to drive the industry forward. Together, we build game-changing products that empower modern real estate entrepreneurs to dominate their markets. From award-winning web design to agile SEO solutions to cutting-edge AI tools, we deliver tech that anticipates market shifts and keeps our clients ahead of their competition.

Founded in 2016 by Stanford Business School alum Malte Kramer, Luxury Presence has grown to a global team ranked on the Inc. 5000 fastest-growing companies list three years in a row. We're backed by world-class investors, including Bessemer Venture Partners, NextEquity Partners, Toba Capital, and Switch Ventures, and have raised $89 million to date.

More than 18,000 real estate businesses rely on our platform, including 30% of the Wall Street Journal RealTrends top agents and teams. Additionally, many of the industry's most powerful brokerages rely on Luxury Presence as a trusted business partner.

Every year since 2020, Luxury Presence has ranked on BuiltIn's Best Place to Work lists. HousingWire named our founder and CEO a 2024 Tech Trendsetter, we've received several Tech100 Awards, and we just scored an Inman Innovation Award for Best AI-Powered Platform.

title: Senior Product Manager - Distribution / Fulfillment  (REMOTE)
desc:
At DICK'S Sporting Goods, we believe in how positively sports can change lives. On our team, everyone plays a critical role in creating confidence and excitement by personally equipping all athletes to achieve their dreams. We are committed to creating an inclusive and diverse workforce, reflecting the communities we serve.

If you are ready to make a difference as part of the world's greatest sports team, apply to join our team today!

OVERVIEW:

Welcome to Product Management at DICK'S Sporting Goods, where we specialize in delivering retail excellence along every aspect of the customer journey.

Our mission focuses on crafting intuitive, easy-to-use products that span across our business. We are looking for a new team member who can bring product-market viability ideas that inspire, innovate and deliver elevated experiences for our customers and products.

The Supply Chain Product organization at DICK'S Sporting Goods is focused on optimizing and innovating our supply chain operations. We are currently seeking a Supply Chain Senior Product Manager to join our team and drive impactful initiatives within our DC Fulfillment Space through technology solutions. In this critical role, you will be responsible for maintaining and delivering a competitive product roadmap, shaping the strategy and vision that drives the success of our supply chain operations. You will have the opportunity to influence and enhance the way we manage our warehousing systems, WMS (Warehouse Management Systems), LMS (Labor Management Systems), and the broader landscape of supply chain technology as we continue to modernize our supply chain network.

Key Considerations for the role:
• Experience with Supply Chain Technology Products - Manhattan Preferred
• In depth operational knowledge of retail distribution operations
• Strong facilitation, leadership, and coordination across technology, capability, and operations teams.
• Strong data acumen
• Strong problem-solving skills with an ability to demonstrate creative thinking to overcome problems

As a Senior Product Manager, you will work closely with other product managers, product designers, software engineers and stakeholders to deliver innovative products and services that align to strategic priorities as well as achieve product-market fit. You will be viewed as a leader within the organization and deliver of high-quality products through the following:

Product Vision & Strategy
The very essence of being a product manager is having a solid foundation to build upon. In this role, you will blend innovation, strategy and execution to deliver top-notch experiences to customers. You will define and establish a comprehensive vision, strategy and roadmap for your product within a modern development environment.

Product Outcomes

Be the "voice of the product" empathizing with customers and stakeholders! As a Senior Product Manager, you will set goals based on outcomes and measure progress towards those goals as well as alignment back to the business. That data then informs future product iterations and prioritization within your roadmap.

Stakeholder Enablement
Bringing cohesion and coordination to domain level initiatives by blending strategy and tactics to achieve business results is essential in this role. You will interact with all functional areas of the company including product design, software engineering, leadership and portfolio stakeholder groups.

Product-Market Fit & Go-to-Market Strategies
You will develop compelling arguments for your product success in the market as well as launch strategies that are inclusive of ramp up, marketing and adoption strategies.

Delivery
Part of the job is to manage the backlog. You will create effective user stories as well as lead backlog prioritization and management; performing testing of product features with engineers to ensure acceptance criteria is met, capture bugs and work towards defect resolution.

QUALIFICATIONS:

Job Duties & Responsibilities

Leadership - How you expand your influence through strategy, decision making, coaching, and management

Enlist & Enable Stakeholders
• Uses evidence-based influencing skills
• Manages complex stakeholder relationships
• Conducts stakeholder identification workshops and creates stakeholder map by product
• Creates and communicates standard stakeholder touchpoints to maintain alignment
• Conducts stakeholder input sessions across various levels within the organization
• Leads Framing exercises and facilitates the enablement of stakeholders

Leadership & People Management
• Acts as a trusted expert, teammate and operations leader within your product domain
• Uses organizational agility to influence teammates and peers without formal authority
• Effectively mobilizes a team(s) and leads through uncertainty
• Acts as an agent of change within the organization
• Creates a people-first culture where teammates thrive
• Advocates for the Lean Product mindset and leads by example
• Actively participates within the Product Community of Practice inclusive of coaching and mentoring responsibilities

Strategy - How you understand and plan within a product team that solves customer problems and drives market impact

Form Strategy & Prioritize Work
• Builds the roadmap, collaboratively with stakeholders, based on outcomes
• Uses decision support tools to weigh the competing factors and objectives for a product(s)
• Effectively explains 'why' you prioritized the work
• Leads the creation of the vision and strategy for the product(s) that you support within a team setting
• Communicates product strategy with dependent teams, stakeholders and leadership
• Develops core positioning and messaging for product, inclusive of developing the product marketing sales tools and collateral

Identify Opportunities
• Uses data to discover customer pain points
• Identifies the right problems and prioritizes for the best business outcomes, aligning to the overarching portfolio strategy
• Identifies complex patterns in data and builds expanded research models in partnership with Product Design
• Uses advanced storytelling to capture the audience and drives alignment / decisions
• Leads Product-Market Fit analysis, segmentation and buyer behavior for a product domain

Form & Track KPIs/Metrics
• Develops growth matrixes, manages product team budget, sets pricing to meet revenue and profitability goals
• Gathers data and conducts research to identify Product ROI
• Evaluates ROI and uses to develop business cases for potential opportunities
• Measures adoption, usage data and business impact of launches to prove ROI
• Builds product objectives and goals in collaboration with key stakeholders
• Drives accountability through facilitated data-driven ROI ceremonies
• Regularly reports out on progress towards goals as well as product health
• Identifies lagging and leading indicators to measure progress towards goals and outcomes

Execution - How you support and provide oversight to the design and delivery of solutions to our customers

As a Senior Product Manager, you may support one or more product teams within a given portfolio.

Deliver Products/Features
• Proactively identifies dependencies and mitigates risky assumptions
• Identifies and communicates risks associated with shipping features
• Builds the release plan (who, what and when) including feature prioritization
• Collaboratively builds the User Story map and corresponding user stories
• Writes user stories to articulate work to be done by software engineering that support outcome delivery
• Works closely with software engineering to ensure delivery of products map to customer experience and business requirements within needed timelines
• Manages testing to detect and fix issues to make necessary improvements
• Manages third party partnerships, assessments and identification of potential opportunities
• Leads sprint planning or iteration planning meetings
• Runs experimental, beta & pilot programs with early-stage products
• Participates in Agile ceremonies, including stand ups, demos and retrospectives
• Communicates with legal, compliance, security and other critical teams

Candidates for this Role Should Have:
• An advanced understanding of Product Management
• Advanced knowledge and application of human-centered design practices
• Understanding of a product lifecycle
• Understanding of the software development lifecycle
• Ability to balance business objectives & customer needs
• Success in defining a cohesive product vision, strategies and roadmap
• Ability to speak to and application of complex product concepts
• Advanced go-to-market strategies resulting in successful product launches
• Customer-centric view into developing great product experiences and the ability to advocate on a customer's behalf
• Use of market data, customer needs and innovative thinking to remain competitive in the domain
• Intermediate understanding of Scrum and application in a Product Management role
• Effective facilitation, relationship building and collaboration skills to drive alignment across organizational peers and stakeholder groups
• Proven ability to influence cross-functional teams and leadership stakeholders without formal authority
• Agility to move between product domains or portfolios as business needs arise
• Advanced written and verbal communication skills
• Curious attitude and desire to learn
• Ability to know when to pivot or persevere based on data and learnings
• Experience working with third party vendors

This is a fully remote position.

This role is not eligible for immigration sponsorship.

#LI-KF1

VIRTUAL REQUIREMENTS:

At DICK'S, we thrive on innovation and authenticity. That said, to protect the integrity and security of our hiring process, we ask that candidates do not use AI tools (like ChatGPT or others) during interviews or assessments.

To ensure a smooth and secure experience, please note the following:
• Cameras must be on during all virtual interviews.
• AI tools are not permitted to be used by the candidate during any part of the interview process.
• Offers are contingent upon a satisfactory background check which may include ID verification.

If you have any questions or need accommodations, we're here to help. Thanks for helping us keep the process fair and secure for everyone!

Targeted Pay Range: $95,200.00 - $158,800.00. This is part of a competitive total rewards package that could include other components such as: incentive, equity and benefits. Individual pay is determined by a number of factors including experience, location, internal pay equity, and other relevant business considerations. We review all teammate pay regularly to ensure competitive and equitable pay.DICK'S Sporting Goods complies with all state paid leave requirements. We also offer a generous suite of benefits. To learn more, visit www.benefityourliferesources.com.

title: Senior Product Manager (Master Data Management)
desc:
Meet Our Team

No‑code and low‑code are more than trends at Pega—they are foundational to how we help the world’s largest enterprises design, build, and scale intelligent applications. As Pega continues to evolve the Pega Infinity™ platform, data, AI, and autonomous agents are becoming the backbone that powers real‑time decisioning, customer engagement, and intelligent automation across every channel.

This role is a hybrid position with a minimum of three days out of our Waltham, Massachusetts office.

Picture Yourself At Pega

In this role, you will shape the future of Pega’s master data and AI‑powered data activation capabilities. You will lead product strategy and execution for data platform services that enable enterprises to unify, govern, reason over, and activate customer and reference data using modern AI techniques and agent‑based architectures.

This position is ideal for a technically strong product manager who has built or owned MDM and/or CDP platforms in complex enterprise environments and is excited about applying AI, AI agents, and modern vibe coding approaches (rapid, iterative, AI‑assisted development) to accelerate platform innovation.

You will act as the connective tissue between engineering, architecture, AI/decisioning teams, applications, and customers—ensuring Pega’s data and AI platform capabilities meet the needs of modern, cloud‑native enterprises.

What You'll Do At Pega
• Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related technical field; advanced degree preferred.
• 7+ years of experience in software product management and/or software engineering, with significant ownership of platform or infrastructure products for large enterprises.
• Proven experience as a Product Manager (or closely related role) for Master Data Management (MDM), Customer Data Platform (CDP), or enterprise data platform products.
• Hands‑on experience with or exposure to platforms such as Informatica, Salesforce Data Cloud, Azure Dataverse, or similar enterprise data solutions.
• Strong understanding of data and AI architecture concepts, including relational and NoSQL data stores, data modeling, metadata management, APIs, event streaming, and real‑time processing.
• Experience working with AI and ML‑enabled systems, such as intelligent matching, recommendations, classification, or decisioning.
• Familiarity with AI agents, orchestration frameworks, or agent‑like architectures that combine data, reasoning, and action.
• Experience working with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP) and modern SaaS delivery models.
• Familiarity with data governance, security, privacy, and compliance requirements in enterprise environments.
• Ability to read technical designs, engage in architectural trade‑off discussions, and challenge assumptions constructively.
• Preferred Skills & Experience
• Prior experience building or scaling AI‑driven data platforms, CDPs, or MDM solutions with embedded intelligence.
• Exposure to generative AI, LLM‑based systems, vector search, or hybrid transactional/analytical platforms.
• Experience working in teams that leverage AI‑assisted development, rapid prototyping, and "vibe coding" approaches.
• Demonstrated executive‑level storytelling: ability to frame strategy, trade‑offs, and investment asks in a crisp narrative for senior leadership and cross‑functional stakeholders.
• Experience leading executive readouts (e.g., QBRs/MBRs), preparing CEO/CPO/CTO briefings, and influencing decision‑makers through clear written docs and compelling presentations.
• Background as a software engineer, data engineer, or architect before transitioning into product management.
• Experience defining data models and APIs consumed by AI agents, decision engines, or autonomous workflows.
• Nice to have: experience engaging external audiences (analysts, partners, conferences) to communicate product strategy and platform differentiation.

Who You Are
• A senior‑level product manager with a strong technical foundation and a passion for building intelligent, enterprise‑grade data and AI platforms.
• Comfortable operating at the intersection of product strategy, deep technical execution, and emerging AI paradigms such as autonomous agents.
• Experienced presenting product vision, strategy, and technical concepts to senior leadership, including executives such as the CEO, Chief Product Officer, CTO, and other C‑level stakeholders.
• An excellent communicator who can clearly articulate complex technical topics to both technical and non‑technical audiences.
• Excited by fast‑moving, iterative product development and modern vibe coding cultures that emphasize experimentation and learning.
• Able to influence and align cross‑functional teams across product management, engineering, architecture, data science, and go‑to‑market functions.
• Confident engaging with highly technical customers, architects, engineers, and data leaders.
• Pragmatic, outcome‑driven, and experienced in balancing long‑term platform investments with near‑term business value.

What You've Accomplished
• Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Software Engineering, or a related technical field; advanced degree preferred.
• 7+ years of experience in software product management and/or software engineering, with significant ownership of platform or infrastructure products for large enterprises.
• Proven experience as a Product Manager (or closely related role) for Master Data Management (MDM), Customer Data Platform (CDP), or enterprise data platform products.
• Hands‑on experience with or exposure to platforms such as Informatica, Salesforce Data Cloud, Azure Dataverse, or similar enterprise data solutions.
• Strong understanding of data and AI architecture concepts, including relational and NoSQL data stores, data modeling, metadata management, APIs, event streaming, and real‑time processing.
• Experience working with AI and ML‑enabled systems, such as intelligent matching, recommendations, classification, or decisioning.
• Familiarity with AI agents, orchestration frameworks, or agent‑like architectures that combine data, reasoning, and action.
• Experience working with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP) and modern SaaS delivery models.
• Familiarity with data governance, security, privacy, and compliance requirements in enterprise environments.
• Ability to read technical designs, engage in architectural trade‑off discussions, and challenge assumptions constructively.

Preferred Skills & Experience
• Prior experience building or scaling AI‑driven data platforms, CDPs, or MDM solutions with embedded intelligence.
• Exposure to generative AI, LLM‑based systems, vector search, or hybrid transactional/analytical platforms.
• Experience working in teams that leverage AI‑assisted development, rapid prototyping, and "vibe coding" approaches.
• Demonstrated executive‑level storytelling: ability to frame strategy, trade‑offs, and investment asks in a crisp narrative for senior leadership and cross‑functional stakeholders.
• Experience leading executive readouts (e.g., QBRs/MBRs), preparing CEO/CPO/CTO briefings, and influencing decision‑makers through clear written docs and compelling presentations.
• Background as a software engineer, data engineer, or architect before transitioning into product management.
• Experience defining data models and APIs consumed by AI agents, decision engines, or autonomous workflows.
• Nice to have: experience engaging external audiences (analysts, partners, conferences) to communicate product strategy and platform differentiation.

Pega Offers You
• Gartner Analyst acclaimed technology leadership across our categories of products
• Continuous learning and development opportunities
• An innovative, inclusive, agile, flexible, and fun work environment
• Competitive global benefits program inclusive of pay + bonus incentive, employee equity in the company

Additional Information

Base salary range for this role is 138,600 - 211,100 USD annually. This role may also be eligible for annual bonus OR commission, as well as benefits and other incentives.

The final compensation will be determined during the offer process based on the candidate's education, experience, skills, and qualifications, as well as market conditions and may vary from the posted range. We will share an information on benefits, bonus/commission, and other pay components for this role at the relevant recruitment stage.

title: Product Manager Vice President - Digital Platform Products for Regulatory & Compliance Controls
desc:
You enjoy shaping the future of product innovation as a core leader, driving value for customers, guiding successful launches, and exceeding expectations. Join our dynamic team and make a meaningful impact by delivering high-quality products that resonate with clients.

As a Product Manager in Commercial & Investment Banking Digital Platform Product team, you are an integral part of the team that innovates new product offerings and leads the end-to-end product life cycle. As a core leader, you are responsible for acting as the voice of the customer and developing profitable products that provide customer value. Utilizing your deep understanding of how to get a product off the ground, you guide the successful launch of products, gather crucial feedback, and ensure top-tier client experiences. With a strong commitment to scalability, resiliency, and stability, you collaborate closely with cross-functional teams to deliver high-quality products that exceed customer expectations.

Job responsibilities
• Develops a product strategy and product vision that delivers value to customers
• Manages discovery efforts and market research to uncover customer solutions and integrate them into the product roadmap
• Owns, maintains, and develops a product backlog that enables development to support the overall strategic roadmap and value proposition
• Builds the framework and tracks the product's key success metrics such as cost, feature and functionality, risk posture, and reliability
• Work with global Control Managers, Legal, Compliance and business managers to ensure that you understand the impact to our product and changes/approvals that are necessary.

title: Product Manager, Complex Assets (Vice President)
desc:
The Complex Assets Product Manager is a senior position within Product Management, responsible for day-to-day product management for complex financial instruments, setting strategy, and providing direction within the Complex Assets domain.

The role requires a deep hands-on working knowledge of Complex Assets, including OTC derivatives, Exchange Traded Derivatives, and Portfolio Swaps, within a financial services business. Additionally, this role will be responsible for the development of product plans, strategies, and tactics while coordinating product lines through product life cycles in coordination with the broader Product Management team.

This role will leverage broad Complex Assets domain knowledge, deep experience influencing product, operations, design, and IT architecture change. Timely execution, seamless business and client change are extremely important to ensure continued growth and expansion of relationships with strategic clients in the complex assets space.

What you’ll do
• Oversee strategic roadmap, development, launch, and marketing to gain maximum benefit from each product
• Oversee day-to-day product management for core products such as product delivery, client experience, and client communication strategies as well as help the team prioritize, negotiate, and remove obstacles to achieve business results
• Execute client value propositions, positioning, segmentation, pricing, targeting, channel strategies, and competitive differentiation to achieve preferred status as a partner to Citi clients
• Develop plans and execute functional strategies for a country, multiple countries, region, or business requiring coordination and integration across units as well as provide input into strategic decisions affecting job family or function within a region or business
• Manage client and competitor market research, develop product innovation roadmap, and address fundamental trials of product commoditization to create an advanced set of solutions
• Oversee all aspects of program life cycle management including market demands, technology trends, and the competitive field
• Contribute to achievement of acquisition targets, product financial performance, revenue performance, and expense management goals as well as oversee identification and execution of opportunities and gaps business plans
• Develop and communicate a business plan to approach the marketplace, and coordinate and implement team procedures, client problem resolutions and client management
• Help drive innovation by working with external partners/alliances to develop products, manage vendor relationships, and prepare agreements
• Appropriately assess risk when business decisions are made, demonstrating particular consideration for the firm's reputation and safeguarding Citigroup, its clients and assets, by driving compliance with applicable laws, rules and regulations, adhering to Policy, applying sound ethical judgment regarding personal behavior, conduct and business practices, and escalating, managing and reporting control issues with transparency, as well as effectively supervise the activity of others and create accountability with those who fail to maintain these standards.

What we’ll need from you
• Extensive Complex Assets/Derivatives industry experience, ideally in a Product Management capacity within financial services.
• Deep and technical understanding of multi-asset class OTC derivatives, including their lifecycle events (intrinsic and extrinsic), with consideration for service level offerings impacting Fund Accounting and Middle Office.
• Proven knowledge of Portfolio Swaps and Exchange Traded Derivatives.
• Demonstrated experience in defining economic minimum data requirements for financial instruments.
• Solid understanding of derivative technical market conventions, data models, and the ability to address market events affecting valuation and processing.
• Previous experience working in Product Management roles focused on financial instruments.
• Experience managing across multi-function and/or global business
• Ability to work under pressure and manage deadlines or unexpected changes in expectations or requirements
• Ability to work unsupervised and adjust priorities quickly as circumstances dictate
• Consistently demonstrates clear and concise written and verbal communication
• Demonstrated analytical skills
• Demonstrated problem-solving and decision-making skills
• Consistently demonstrates clear and concise written and verbal communication
• Ability to work in a team-oriented environment

title: Product Manager - Product Engineering
desc:
A Product Manager is a senior individual contributor responsible for ensuring a product's value and viability within a product portfolio. This role involves leading empowered, cross-functional product teams to solve moderate complexity customer problems that align with high value business needs. The Product Manager is accountable for the product's success, from vision to execution, and collaborates closely with various functions and stakeholders to deliver valuable, viable, usable, and feasible solutions.

Recruiting for this role ends on 4/30/2026.

Key Responsibilities
• Product Accountability
• Responsible and accountable for the product's value and viability, including profit and loss.
• Formulate and achieve Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) for identified problems to solve.
• Drive strategy-aligned solutions to achieve product profit and loss objectives.
• Measure KPIs and analyze outcomes to inform future strategies.
• Vision and Strategy
• Co-create, own, and evangelize the product vision, strategy, and roadmap.
• Align product objectives with the product portfolio and business goals.
• Co-create in collaboration with business stakeholders, engineering, experience, and delivery.
• Market and User Engagement
• Conduct user research and competitive analysis.
• Engage the team with users and stakeholders through continuous research and direct interactions.
• Collaborate and guide the team toward solutions that address priority user and business needs.
• Apply analytical skills to analyze data and derive actionable insights.
• Adopt innovative and experimental approaches to solving complex problems.
• Collaboration and Teamwork
• Work side-by-side with cross-functional (business, engineering, experience, and delivery) team members to achieve KPI outcomes.
• Promote a product operating model that emphasizes outcomes over output (minimize overproduction while maximizing value).
• Build empowered teams and product communities who exhibit collective product ownership.
• Continuous Improvement
• Remove obstacles for the team and ensure smooth flow of continuous value achievement.
• Promote and drive rapid, emergent, and ongoing learning and adaptation to meet objectives.
• Drive innovation and improvement of the process to drive out waste and accelerate value achievement.
• Spread knowledge and best practices within the product vertical community.

Required Qualifications
• Bachelor's degree in business, Marketing, Engineering, or a related field.
• 6+ years of proven experience in lean product management or related roles.
• 3+ years of enterprise scale experience across multiple business areas.
• Limited immigration sponsorship may be available
• Ability to travel 0-20%, on average, based on the work you do and the clients and industries/sectors you serve

Preferred Qualifications
• Preferred Education: An MBA or related advanced degree is preferred.
• Preferred Experience:
• Demonstrated experience in modern product craft of delivering the right thing, in the right way, at the right time. Significant experience in lean product management craft and domain (tools, methods, and practices). Seen as a leader in this space.
• Demonstrated experience leveraging AI to increase product management effectiveness (e.g., accelerating discovery synthesis, writing/communication) with responsible-use judgment.
• Experience building or evolving AI-enabled applications
• Proven accountability for value, viability and P&L objectives for a product and for an empowered product team.
• Preferred Skills
• Communication: Clear and effective communication with team members, stakeholders, and customers. Excellent communication and collaboration abilities.
• Leadership: Ability to lead and inspire cross-functional teams, fostering collaboration and collective movement toward product goals. Ability to influence at all organizational levels through inclusion and leadership.
• Customer-Centricity: Deep understanding of customer needs and engagement patterns, driving teams to deliver solutions that customers love and that work for the business. Expertise in applying customer-centric methods and practices.
• Strategic Thinking: Ability to develop and execute a strategic vision for the product, aligning it with broader business objectives.
• Exceptional analytical and problem-solving skills.
• Detail-oriented, organized, and visionary.
• Learning-forward, experimental, and value-oriented mindset.
• Ability to navigate complexity and uncertainty.
• Quick to reach expert-level knowledge within the product domain being served.
• Preferred Personal Traits
• Strong leadership capabilities.
• Customer-centric mindset.
• Ability to work as an individual contributor in a collaborative, cross-functional team.
• Humble, curious, and learning-forward mindset.
• Favor small step action and evidence over detailed upfront planning and precision aiming.
• Experience with lean solutions and rapid, inexpensive experimentation to emerge the right thing, int the right way, at the right time.
• High levels of continuous customer and user engagement.

Conclusion

The Product Manager plays a crucial role in ensuring the success of our high value, moderately complex products by balancing customer needs with business objectives. This role requires a blend of strategic vision, analytical skills, and collaborative teamwork to deliver valuable, viable, usable, and feasible solutions. It demands significant experience in the modern product management craft and a drive for continuous improvement.

The wage range for this role takes into account the wide range of factors that are considered in making compensation decisions including but not limited to skill sets; experience and training; licensure and certifications; and other business and organizational needs. The disclosed range estimate has not been adjusted for the applicable geographic differential associated with the location at which the position may be filled. At Deloitte, it is not typical for an individual to be hired at or near the top of the range for their role and compensation decisions are dependent on the facts and circumstances of each case. A reasonable estimate of the current range is $102,500 to $210,600.

You may also be eligible to participate in a discretionary annual incentive program, subject to the rules governing the program, whereby an award, if any, depends on various factors, including, without limitation, individual and organizational performance.

title: Product Manager
desc:
Step into a role where you own the future of connected healthcare solutions. Our client is seeking a Product Manager who will shape strategy, guide innovation from concept to commercialization, and directly influence how patients and providers connect through technology-driven products. This is a high-impact opportunity to lead products that drive real clinical and commercial outcomes.

Why You Should Apply
• Own the full product lifecycle with true business ownership
• Influence strategy, roadmap decisions, and global product direction
• Collaborate cross-functionally with Engineering, Quality, Sales, and Marketing leaders
• On-site role with meaningful customer and international exposure (25–40% travel)
• Salary Range: 120-140K
• 15% annual bonus target and full benefits

What You’ll Be Doing
• Own product roadmap, positioning, and market strategy
• Lead opportunity assessment and prioritize innovation initiatives
• Build business plans, pricing strategies, and profitability analysis
• Translate customer needs into product requirements and development plans
• Drive NPI planning, launches, and commercial adoption programs
• Define KPIs and measure adoption, growth, and performance

About You
• Be able to do the job as described.
• Act with an ownership mindset as the “CEO” of a product line

title: Principal Product Manager
desc:
Overview

We are seeking a Principal Product Manager for Skrill—a senior, highly strategic leader responsible for driving enterprise‑critical product initiatives and shaping Paysafe’s long‑term platform direction. In this role, you will leverage deep domain expertise, strong cross‑functional leadership, and the ability to influence decisions at the highest levels of the organization. You will define the product vision for complex, multi‑team capabilities and guide Product Managers and Engineering leaders to deliver scalable, innovative payment solutions.

This position is ideal for someone who operates as a thought leader, a strategic problem solver, and a connector across organizational boundaries. The role follows a hybrid work model, requiring three days per week onsite in our Jacksonville, Florida office.

Key Responsibilities

Strategic Product Leadership
• Own the long-term strategy for a major platform capability, cross-product domain, or enterprise-level initiative.
• Build and champion a clear vision that aligns to Paysafe’s multi-year business goals.
• Identify market shifts, emerging technologies, and regulatory trends to shape proactive product evolution.
• Develop high-impact business cases to secure executive alignment and investment.

Cross-Functional Alignment & Influence
• Act as a senior partner to Engineering, Architecture, Compliance, Risk, Operations, and Commercial Leadership.
• Drive alignment across multiple product lines, ensuring cohesive prioritization and technical direction.
• Present strategy, progress, and recommendations to senior leadership and executive teams.

Complex Program Leadership
• Break down large, ambiguous problem spaces into clear, actionable initiatives for multiple teams.
• Guide Product Managers and Product Owners in defining epics, features, goals, and dependencies.
• Anticipate organizational, technical, regulatory, and operational challenges early and create mitigation strategies.
• Ensure cross-team delivery success for mission-critical initiatives.

Customer, Market & Industry Expertise
• Deeply understand customer needs, competitive dynamics, partner ecosystems, and payment industry trends.
• Establish the problem definition and strategic framing for entire product domains.
• Represent Paysafe externally with partners, clients, and industry groups as a domain expert.

Technical & Architectural Leadership
• Partner with engineering and architecture to define and evolve platform capabilities, integration strategies, and technical roadmaps.
• Ensure long-term scalability, reliability, and compliance considerations are embedded in product direction.
• Challenge technical and business assumptions to guide sustainable solutions.

Thought Leadership & Mentorship
• Mentor and coach Product Managers, Senior Product Managers, and Product Owners across the organization.
• Elevate product management standards, tools, and processes.
• Contribute to product culture, organizational development, and strategic planning.

Qualifications
• Bachelor’s degree in Business, Economics, Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field, required (or additional experience in lieu of education); MBA preferred.
• 10+ years of experience in product management, with deep experience in payments, fintech, or large-scale B2B platforms.
• Demonstrated success defining strategy and delivering complex, cross-functional product initiatives.
• Strong ability to influence and communicate with senior executives.
• Advanced understanding of payment systems, risk, compliance, APIs, platform architecture, or related technical domains.
• Exceptional strategic thinking, structured problem solving, and analytical ability.
• Experience mentoring PMs and driving product excellence across teams.

What You Bring
• A visionary mindset with the ability to turn ambiguity into a clear, compelling product direction.
• Deep expertise in payment industry dynamics, regulatory shifts, and customer needs.
• Strong leadership presence and the ability to influence without authority.
• A passion for elevating the practice of product management at Paysafe.
• High ownership, accountability, and a track record of driving meaningful business impact.

title: Technology Product Manager, Associate Director - APIs, Integrations, and Platform Capabilities
desc:
Senior Individual Technical Product Manager, Healthcare APIs, Integrations, and Platform Capabilities
• Hybrid - from approved Wolters Kluwer Office

Role Overview

UpToDate (UTD) is a trusted global clinical decision support platform used by clinicians around the world.

The Technology Product Manager, Associate Director is an individual contributor role to lead product direction for APIs, integrations, and advanced platform capabilities that embed UpToDate into third-party clinical systems.

This role focuses on enabling secure, reliable, and scalable integration of UpToDate content and intelligence into EHRs, health systems, and other clinical environments. You will define the requirements these capabilities place on shared platform services and partner closely with platform product and engineering teams to ensure alignment.

This role directly influences how UpToDate shows up outside our core application. You will shape the integration layer that partners build on while influencing the underlying platform capabilities required to support them. Success means building capabilities that are widely adopted, operationally sound, and increasingly mature in their reliability, governance, and production readiness.

This role is well suited for someone comfortable operating in ambiguity, biased toward action, and effective in deeply cross-functional, technical, and regulated environments.

Responsibilities
• Own product direction for APIs, integrations, and advanced platform capabilities that embed UpToDate into external clinical workflows.
• Define clear product requirements for authentication, authorization, data exchange, workflow triggers, observability, reliability, scalability, and operational readiness.
• Translate clinical, regulatory, and enterprise expectations into actionable product requirements and technical tradeoffs.
• Shape roadmap sequencing and influence shared platform priorities through clearly articulated requirements and decisions.
• Make tradeoffs across scope, timing, and technical approach when multiple teams and partners depend on shared foundations.
• Establish standards for API lifecycle management, governance, versioning, and developer experience.
• Drive the ongoing maturity of integration and platform solutions, improving reliability, monitoring, risk management, and production scalability.
• Drive measurable adoption, reliability, and partner success through high-quality, workflow-integrated platform capabilities.
• Partner closely with engineering, architecture, security, privacy, and legal stakeholders to ensure responsible scaling in regulated clinical environments.
• Support integration discussions with business development and strategic partners and contribute to customer-facing conversations where platform capabilities intersect with clinical workflow needs.
• Contribute to rollout planning, risk mitigation, and continuous improvement of platform capabilities at scale.

Qualifications
• 8+ years delivering complex software products end-to-end, from problem framing through launch and iteration, including platform, API-first, developer-facing, or integration-heavy products.
• 2+ years owning product direction for a platform capability, API surface, or shared technical system at a senior level.
• Strong technical fluency, with the ability to reason about APIs, distributed systems, authentication and authorization models, event-driven architectures, reliability concepts, and cloud-based systems. Able to engage credibly in architectural discussions and translate technical complexity into clear product decisions.
• Demonstrated success defining and evolving integration platforms that developers adopt, including API design principles, lifecycle management, versioning strategies, governance standards, documentation, and developer experience.
• Experience delivering AI-enabled capabilities in production environments, including defining requirements for evaluation, monitoring, guardrails, and operational readiness.
• Strong product judgment in enterprise or high-trust contexts, balancing partner needs, platform constraints, clinical and regulatory considerations, and business impact.
• Ability to lead through influence across engineering, architecture, security, compliance, and commercial stakeholders in regulated environments.
• Experience in healthcare or other regulated domains strongly preferred; familiarity with EHR integrations and healthcare interoperability standards is a plus.
• Prior experience in a technical role such as software engineering or solutions architecture is a plus.
• Experience collaborating with business development or strategic partnership teams preferred.

Our Interview Practices

To maintain a fair and genuine hiring process, we kindly ask that all candidates participate in interviews without the assistance of AI tools or external prompts. Our interview process is designed to assess your individual skills, experiences, and communication style. We value authenticity and want to ensure we're getting to know you-not a digital assistant. To help maintain this integrity, we ask to remove virtual backgrounds and include in-person interviews in our hiring process. Please note that use of AI-generated responses or third-party support during interviews will be grounds for disqualification from the recruitment process.

Applicants may be required to appear onsite at a Wolters Kluwer office as part of the recruitment process.

Compensation:
$160,100.00 - $286,000.00 USD

This role is eligible for Bonus.

Compensation range listed is based on primary location of the position. Actual base salary offer is influenced by a wide array of factors including but not limited to skills, experience and actual hiring location. Your recruiter can share more information about the specific offer for the job location during the hiring process.

Additional Information:

Wolters Kluwer offers a wide variety of competitive benefits and programs to help meet your needs and balance your work and personal life, including but not limited to: Medical, Dental, & Vision Plans, 401(k), FSA/HSA, Commuter Benefits, Tuition Assistance Plan, Vacation and Sick Time, and Paid Parental Leave.
